title: Floating-point Representations and IEEE 754
summary: Explains scientific notation in base 2, the structure of IEEE 754 single and double precision, special values (NaN, ∞, denormalised), and representable intervals.
content_points: Normalized base-2 form: 1.f × 2^e; the leading 1 is implicit (not stored).
Components: sign S (1 bit), characteristic C = e + excess (biased exponent), fractional mantissa f.
Single precision (32 bits): S=1, C=8 (excess-127), f=23. Range ≈ ±1.2×10^(-38) to ±3.4×10^38.
Double precision (64 bits): S=1, C=11 (excess-1023), f=52. Range ≈ ±1.7×10^(-308) to ±1.7×10^308.
Special values: infinity (C=max, f=0), NaN (C=max, f≠0), denormalized (C=min, f≠0), zero (C=min, f=0).
learning_objectives: Describe the structure of IEEE 754 single and double precision
